join_order.output: partkey_bitset[2000001] (flat bool/bitset array, direct index by p_partkey)
nation_name[25] string array; nation_sort_idx[25] for stable ordering

join_order.layout: uint32_t ht_size=16777216; then ht_size x {int64_t key, uint32_t offset, uint32_t count} (16 bytes/slot); then 8000000 x uint32_t row_idx
join_order.hash_fn: uint64_t h = composite_key * 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15ULL; h ^= h >> 33; return h & mask

join_order.layout: uint32_t ht_size=33554432; then ht_size x {int32_t key, uint32_t offset, uint32_t count} (12 bytes/slot); then 15000000 x uint32_t row_idx
join_order.purpose: probe with l_orderkey -> o_row_idx -> o_orderdate -> extract_year

per_row_pipeline: check partkey_bitset[l_partkey] — skip if false (~94.7% skip)
probe partsupp_keys_hash with (l_partkey<<32 | (uint32_t)l_suppkey) — bounded for-loop, probe < ht_size — get ps_row_idx
probe orders_orderkey_hash with l_orderkey — bounded for-loop — get o_row_idx
year = extract_year(o_orderdate) using date_utils.h (requires init_date_tables() at startup)
amount = l_extendedprice*(1.0 - l_discount) - ps_supplycost*l_quantity  (all native double, no scale factor)

join_order.method: sequential_sum over 64 thread-local double[175] arrays into final double[175]
std::sort on 175-element vector of (nation_string, year, sum_profit)

data_structures.part_filter: flat_array bool[2000001] (partkey_bitset, direct index by partkey, ~2MB, cache-friendly for lineitem probe)
data_structures.nation_lookup: direct_array std::string[25] nation_name indexed by nationkey (25 entries)
data_structures.partsupp_join: pre_built_index mmap (partsupp_keys_hash.bin, 16M slots x 16 bytes = 256MB, zero build cost)
data_structures.orders_join: pre_built_index mmap (orders_orderkey_hash.bin, 33M slots x 12 bytes = 384MB, zero build cost)
data_structures.aggregation: direct_array double[175] per thread (nation_idx*7 + (year-1992)), 64 thread-local copies

indexes.purpose: O(1) lookup (l_partkey, l_suppkey) -> ps_row_idx -> ps_supplycost, zero build cost
O(1) lookup l_orderkey -> o_row_idx -> o_orderdate, zero build cost

date_handling.rule: C1+C11: call gendb::init_date_tables() once at top of main(); use extract_year(o_orderdate) from date_utils.h

correctness_checklist: C1: date_utils.h for o_orderdate epoch-day extraction
C11: gendb::init_date_tables() at top of main()
C20: std::fill (not memset) for INT32_MIN/INT64_MIN sentinel initialization in any runtime-built tables
C24: bounded for-loop probing (probe < ht_size) in all hash index lookups
C22: use exact DB path from Query Guide: /home/jl4492/GenDB/benchmarks/tpc-h/gendb/sf10.gendb
